commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The blocking code defect is a narrow mechanical capture-state repair; real Slack proof remains a separate contributor or maintainer action.; Needs attention: The capture fallback can store content that a delivery hook removed or redacted.; Review finding: [P1] Preserve finals removed by delivery hooks — src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts:839 (sha=85add3b017853dcc882bfaffcf5e37e407a59adf)
commands.review_summary:
The PR extends final-reply transcript mirroring to successful same-channel Slack dispatcher deliveries and adds focused regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +17, Tests +35. Total +52 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: same-channel Slack final replies bypass routed-reply mirroring, and the focused dispatcher harness reaches that branch. A live current-main Slack reproduction was not performed.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The blocking code defect is a narrow mechanical capture-state repair; real Slack proof remains a separate contributor or maintainer action.

target.title: fix(reply): mirror same-channel Slack final replies
target.body:
## Summary
- Mirror same-surface external final replies into the owning session transcript after dispatcher delivery succeeds.
- Reuse the existing final-outcome guard so cancelled or failed dispatcher deliveries are not mirrored.
- Add regression coverage for the Slack same-channel path from #92489.

## Issue
Fixes #92489

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Slack channel/thread turns where `Provider` already matches `OriginatingChannel` should still persist delivered assistant final replies to the owning session transcript, even though they do not use the routed-reply path.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `85add3b017853dcc882bfaffcf5e37e407a59adf` on macOS, Node v24.15.0, running the real `dispatchReplyFromConfig` test harness for Slack same-channel dispatch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm exec vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts --testNamePattern 'mirrors same-channel Slack final replies into the session transcript' --reporter=verbose`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied terminal output from the focused run:

```text
RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/andy/openclaw-92489

✓ |auto-reply| ../../src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts > dispatchReplyFromConfig > mirrors same-channel Slack final replies into the session transcript 9ms

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  1 passed | 201 skipped (202)
Duration  3.22s
```

- Observed result after fix: The same-channel Slack final reply path completed without calling routed reply delivery and appended the delivered assistant reply to `agent:main:slack:channel:C123` with agent id `main` and the expected reply text.
- What was not tested: A live Slack workspace/API end-to-end run was not performed in this environment.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The proof uses the repository's dispatch-layer Slack harness to exercise the exact same-channel path without external Slack credentials.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this patch, transcript mirroring was only attached to source-reply metadata/routed delivery, so same-channel Slack finals could be delivered without appending an assistant transcript entry.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm exec vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` - 202 tests passed.
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` - passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --write src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts && git diff --check` - passed.
- `pnpm tsgo:prod` - passed.
- `pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:compile` - passed; 114 plugins compiled.
target.author: turbotheturtle

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
